title: Wire frustration detector to help UI — show nudge toast when user is stuck
description: frustration-detector.ts tracks behavior (rapid undo, pauses, failed attempts) and computes frustration levels (calm/mild/frustrated/very-frustrated) but is NOT connected to any UI. When level reaches 'frustrated', show a non-intrusive toast: 'Stuck? Try browsing templates (Cmd+T)' or 'Need help? Check the hint panel.' When 'very-frustrated', offer to replay onboarding tour. Uses existing notification-store for toast delivery.
acceptanceCriteria: When frustration level reaches 'mild': subtle hint appears in status bar
When 'frustrated': toast notification with actionable suggestion
When 'very-frustrated': offer to replay onboarding or open help
Toast is non-blocking and dismissible
Respects user preference (can disable in settings)
Frustration resets on successful action (prevents spam)

title: Add info tooltips to simulation metric pills explaining what each number means
description: SimulationDashboard shows RPS, P99 Latency, Error Rate, Cost/hr as metric pills with no explanation. A beginner doesn't know if '200ms P99' is good or bad. Add hover tooltips to each metric pill: RPS: 'Requests per second — how many requests the system handles. Higher is better.' P99 Latency: '99% of requests complete under this time. Under 200ms is good for most APIs.' Error Rate: 'Percentage of requests that fail. Under 1% is healthy.' Cost/hr: 'Estimated infrastructure cost at current load.'
acceptanceCriteria: Each metric pill in SimulationDashboard has a hover tooltip
Tooltip explains: what the metric measures, what good/bad values look like
Tooltips use beginner-friendly language (no jargon)
Tooltips don't interfere with the compact dashboard layout

title: Add first-encounter contextual tooltips for heatmap toggle and time-travel scrubber
description: Existing first-encounter system tracks 4 features (canvas-drag, palette-open, simulation-play, export-open) using useFirstEncounter hook. Two powerful features lack discovery: (1) Heatmap toggle — just a flame icon, no label, no tooltip on first use. (2) Time-travel scrubber — appears after simulation without introduction. Add 2 new first-encounter entries: 'heatmap-first-toggle' and 'time-travel-first-appear'. Same pattern as existing contextual tooltips.
acceptanceCriteria: First time heatmap toggle appears: contextual tooltip explains 'Color-code nodes by utilization, latency, or error rate'
First time time-travel scrubber appears: tooltip explains 'Scrub through the simulation timeline. Set breakpoints by clicking.'
Both use existing ContextualTooltip component with useFirstEncounter hook
Both persist in localStorage so they only show once
Both auto-dismiss after 5 seconds

title: Add 'Replay Onboarding Tour' button in Settings panel
description: Onboarding overlay can be skipped but cannot be replayed. Users who skipped and later want guidance have no way to see the tour again. Add a 'Replay Onboarding Tour' button in the Settings panel. On click: set onboardingActive=true and onboardingStep=0 in ui-store, close settings, show onboarding overlay.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Replay Onboarding Tour' button visible in Settings panel
Clicking it closes settings and starts the 5-step tour from step 1
Tour completion re-sets localStorage key
Button is accessible via keyboard

title: Upgrade empty state with animated preview and inspiring copy
description: Current empty state: LayoutDashboard icon + 'Start Building' + 'Drag components...' text + 2 buttons. Functional but not inspiring. Upgrade: (1) Replace static icon with a small looping animation showing particles flowing through a mini architecture (3-4 nodes), (2) Change heading to 'Design systems like Netflix and Uber — then watch them run under load', (3) Add a third CTA: 'Try a Quick Demo' that auto-loads URL Shortener + starts simulation. The empty state should make users EXCITED, not just informed.
acceptanceCriteria: Empty state has animated preview (particles flowing through mini architecture)
Inspiring heading that communicates the platform's unique value
3 CTAs: Browse Templates, Quick Demo, Cmd+K
'Quick Demo' auto-loads URL Shortener template and starts simulation
Animation respects prefers-reduced-motion (shows static preview instead)

title: Add 'Start Here' badge and beginner section to template gallery
description: 55 templates shown with no indication of which to try first. A beginner opening the gallery is overwhelmed. Add: (1) 'Start Here' badge on URL Shortener and Rate Limiter (difficulty 2), (2) Pinned 'Beginner's Path' section at top of gallery showing 3 recommended first templates with brief 'why start here' text, (3) Section collapses after user has loaded 3+ templates.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Start Here' badge visible on 2-3 beginner templates
Pinned 'Beginner's Path' section at top of gallery with 3 templates
Each recommended template has 1-sentence 'why start here' text
Section auto-collapses after user has loaded 3+ templates (localStorage)
Badge styling is distinct and eye-catching

title: Add ChaosQuickBar first-encounter tooltip explaining chaos injection
description: ChaosQuickBar appears during simulation but has no introduction. Users see categorized chaos events (Infrastructure, Network, Data, etc.) but don't know they need to DRAG them onto nodes. Add first-encounter tooltip: 'Inject failures into your system! Drag any chaos event onto a node to see how your architecture handles it. Try dragging "Cache Failure" onto the Redis node.'
acceptanceCriteria: First-encounter tooltip appears when ChaosQuickBar first becomes visible
Tooltip explains: what it is, how to use it (drag onto node), suggests first action
Uses existing useFirstEncounter pattern with 'chaos-first-appear' key
Auto-dismisses after 8 seconds
Only shows once (persisted in localStorage)
